message.content:
Try HARD to REFUTE this plan through ONE lens. Default refuted=true if uncertain. Check /home/polaris/polaris_project/drb_corpus/gpt55_board and /home/polaris/wt/flywheel.

DIAGNOSIS: FOUR MEASURED CAUSES + TWO STRATEGIC ERRORS. Measured: (1) ATTRIBUTION INVISIBLE — all 240 [n] markers and the reference list are deleted by the RACE cleaner before judging (cleaner_survival_test.py: 0/12 survival), so the judge reads naked assertions on a task whose instruction is 'only cites high-quality, English-language journal articles'; (2) CROSS-SOURCE INFERENCE BANNED BY CONSTRUCTION — entailment_judge.py NEUTRAL clause ('introduces a fact, entity, mechanism, or specificity NOT present in the SPAN', /home/polaris/wt/outline_agent/src/polaris_graph/llm/entailment_judge.py) plus synthesis_contract.py:197-205 delete exactly the sentence class that wins INSIGHT (weight 0.32, our worst dim 0.4238); (3) PHYSICALLY UNREADABLE — 12 paragraphs, median 677 words, 0 H3 (verified vs rank10_sections_compose/report.md); readability 0.3774 is worst on the board and bleeds into the single-call judging of all other dims; (4) CORPUS MIS-AIMED — 997 rows/919 URLs but only ~17-22 on-topic journal articles, 5 rows with authors, grey-lit top domains; the PG_AGENTIC loop was never pointed at this task. Strategic errors in the brief that this plan corrects: (A) UNITS — 0.5578 is cellcog's 100-task corpus-wide score; cellcog has NO task-72 raw_results; regression estimate of its task-72 is ~0.556±0.019, and the highest MEASURED task-72 score is bodhi's 0.5441. (B) INVERSION — the brief frames cellcog's un-sourced synthesis as a weakness to out-verify; it is the engineered strength behind insight 0.5708 at weight 0.32 (a dedicated 'Contested Debates and Novel Insights' section of 8 named, epistemically tagged syntheses 'no single paper articulates'). The winning play is: copy cellcog's insight engine and monograph architecture, keep our deterministic no-fabrication floor underneath it, and out-gun it on evidence volume/recency/method-texture where the rubric verbatim pays for 'Data and Factual Support' and 'Depth and Representativeness of Literature Synthesized'.
RETRIEVAL: JOURNAL-FIRST AGENTIC LOOP + CITATION-GRAPH SNOWBALL, STOPPED BY A COVERAGE MATRIX, NOT A COUNT. (a) QUERY-GEN: run the existing planner perspectives (/home/polaris/wt/outline_agent/src/polaris_graph/agents/planner.py:309-531, PG_AGENTIC_SEED_QUERIES) but seed perspectives FROM the coverage matrix below, one per cell-group; queries phrased as scholarly-literature queries ('meta-analysis robot adoption employment', 'algorithmic management working conditions journal'), not news queries. (b) SEARCH: enable the idle loop — PG_AGENTIC_SEARCH_ENABLED=1 (/home/polaris/wt/outline_agent/src/polaris_graph/state.py:219), PG_AGENTIC_MAX_ROUNDS=15 (state.py:220, default 12), PG_AGENTIC_MAX_QUERIES=160 (state.py:221); INVERT the channel mix: PG_AGENTIC_ACADEMIC_PER_ROUND 2→8 and PG_AGENTIC_WEB_PER_ROUND 6→2 (state.py:229-230) so OpenAlex/Semantic-Scholar-class results dominate rounds (loop entry searcher.py:291). (c) EXPANSION — the decisive stage keyword search provably cannot do (Autor-Levy-Murnane's title contains neither 'AI' nor 'labor market'): after every 3 rounds, feed all newly found DOIs into /home/polaris/wt/flywheel/scripts/journal_corpus_build.py citation-graph expansion, BOTH directions (references + cited-by via Crossref/OpenAlex), depth 2 from canonical anchors (Autor-Levy-Murnane 2003; Acemoglu-Restrepo 2020; Frey-Osborne; Brynjolfsson-Mitchell-Rock; the 2024-25 meta-analyses). (d) FETCH: journal_corpus_fetch.py + deep_fetch.py (Crossref abstracts + Unpaywall OA full text); require retrievable abstract-or-fulltext, else keep metadata-only for context citations. (e) SELECT: hard gate = has DOI + journal + AUTHOR NAMES (the old corpus had 5/997 with authors — automatic reject now) + English; LLM on-topic gate scoring title+abstract against the task text (kills ResNet and Cognitive Psychology 1974); working papers (NBER/SSRN) quarantined into a 'contextual-only' bin, never primary evidence — this reproduces cellcog's Webb move. (f) WEIGHT: quality = journal standing + citation count + recency bonus (2024-2026, cellcog's refs are 41/104 from 2024+) + method-texture availability (does the abstract state N, design, identification?). (g) DEDUP: DOI-canonical; fuzzy-title for DOI-less. (h) CONSOLIDATE into evidence cards (next layer). STOPPING CONDITION = COVERAGE MATRIX, not a number: rows = restructuring dimensions the task names (employment/displacement, wages, skills/task content, polarization, autonomy/surveillance/algorithmic management, labor share, gender, Global South/informality, institutions/policy) x industries (manufacturing, healthcare, finance, retail, transport/logistics, professional services, education, platform/gig) x eras (computerization, robots/narrow AI, generative AI) x levels (platform/firm/regional/national/informal-sector). STOP when every reachable cell holds >=2 on-topic journal works OR a declared literature gap (gaps get narrated as findings — 'the peer-reviewed evidence on X is thin because...', which cellcog does and which no faithfulness gate can cut), AND total >=120 on-topic journal works with >=35% dated 2024+, AND one full round adds <2 new on-topic works per 20 queries. Target 120-150 works vs cellcog's ~100 — evidence volume is the lane where we out-score rather than match.
SYNTHESIS: THE CORRECTED-CORRECTED LINE. The brief's fix ('a mechanism may be asserted if ANY source in the corpus states it AND we cite that source') is necessary but NOT sufficient — grounding proved it would delete cellcog's highest-value section (§5 opens: 'syntheses that no single paper in the peer-reviewed literature articulates'), and 71% of cellcog's sentences carry no citation at all. FOUR LEGAL SENTENCE CLASSES: (1) GROUNDED FACT — asserts what a cited card's span states; entailment as before. (2) COMPOSITE BRIDGE (bodhi's winning move) — finding from paper A + mechanism from paper B, BOTH cited; fix /home/polaris/wt/flywheel/scripts/synthesis_contract.py:197-205 so the mechanism check runs against the declared mechanisms of ANY cited card in the corpus, not only the two premises being compared. (3) ADJUDICATION — cited finding A + cited finding B + an uncited, HEDGED resolution that names the resolving dimension drawn from declared card fields (level/horizon/method): 'The parallel is not a contradiction but a demonstration: firm-level aggregates can show growth while exposed workers face losses.' Legal iff the named dimension differs between the premise cards — deterministically checkable. (4) LABELED ANALYTICAL SYNTHESIS (cellcog's insight engine, the class the brief's rule bans) — an un-sourced mechanism/claim is legal IFF all three hold: (i) HEDGED verb from an allowlist ('may', 'could', 'we propose as analytical synthesis', 'suggests'); (ii) EPISTEMICALLY TAGGED with the graded taxonomy ([Established Finding] / [Emerging Pattern] / [Conceptual Argument] / [Analytical Hypothesis / Our Synthesis]); (iii) accompanied by an EXPLICIT GAP DECLARATION verified against the gap index ('No peer-reviewed study has yet measured...' — and our corpus confirms none does). That is cellcog's fabrication-free mechanism verbatim: assert the original claim AND tell the judge it is original and untested. STILL DELETED, DETERMINISTICALLY, ALWAYS: any number not in a source span; any study/organisation/author that does not exist; any attribution to someone who did not say it; any UNHEDGED-UNTAGGED mechanism no card states; forecasts stated as fact (hedged scenario-discussion tagged [Analytical Hypothesis] is legal; lunon's '2030 verdict' style is not); universals. The entailment judge's NEUTRAL clause stops being a sentence-killer and becomes a ROUTER: NEUTRAL sentences route to contract classes 2-4 instead of deletion.
COMPOSER: EXTEND /home/polaris/wt/flywheel/scripts/cellcog_composer.py TO THE MEASURED #1 ARCHITECTURE — cellcog's actual moves, not the brief's misreadings. (1) CITATION TEMPLATE — cellcog's real workhorse (73 instances), NOT the brief's 'Writing in the JEP in 2019' contortion: 'Author(s) (YYYY), in the *Journal Name*, [verb] that ...' — author name and journal name in prose, YEAR STAYS IN PARENS (160/177 of cellcog's year-parens are this narrative form and it wins; the brief's 'year MUST be prose' rule is refuted). Zero [n] in body; references section retained for provenance (cleaner deletes it; costs nothing). (2) SPINE = THREE TECHNOLOGY ERAS (computerization/polarization → robots/narrow AI → generative AI) with sectors NESTED inside each era — cellcog's actual skeleton. 4IR handled by critique-and-subordinate: adopt as periodization in §2, contest it with journal-published critiques, decline the frame uncritically; NO four-revolutions comparison spine (that is WhaleCloud's move, 0.5396). (3) STRUCTURE: ~10 H2 + ~30 H3 + 6-8 H4; median paragraph ~100w, max <280w; body 12,500-14,000 words (cellcog 13,486; any 8k cap fights #1 with half its ammunition). (4) STRUCTURED ABSTRACT Objective/Methods/Findings/Contributions with a THESIS stated up front — our analogue of cellcog's 'Heterogeneity, not displacement, is the central peer-reviewed finding', derived from our contradiction index; Contributions line enumerates the syntheses by count. (5) SCOPE/METHODS/SOURCE-SELECTION section that ARGUES compliance: narrates journal-only criteria, analyzes the constraint as a publication bias with a stated compensation strategy, pre-empts the working-paper objection — plus point-of-use re-enactment whenever a contextual-only card appears ('Because X remains a working paper, we reference it contextually rather than as primary evidence'). (6) THE INSIGHT ENGINE: a dedicated 'Contested Debates and Novel Insights' H2 with 6-8 NAMED, BRANDED syntheses ('the informality buffer', 'the gender exposure reversal' style), each opened with first-person ownership, epistemically tagged via the graded taxonomy, hedged, and gap-declared — a numbered inventory of insights handed to the INSIGHT judge (weight 0.32). Include the legend paragraph: 'The convention is not cosmetic...'. (7) ONE MASTER KEY threaded through everything — level-of-analysis/institutional mediation at five named levels — so the document has a thesis, not coverage. (8) MONOGRAPH WIRING: ~30 internal cross-references ('a tension taken up in Section 5.3') — zero board systems except cellcog do this; cheap to generate in a second pass. (9) EPIGRAMMATIC PARAGRAPH CLOSES: unit of composition = evidence → evidence → one-line citation-free abstraction. (10) OPENING/CLOSING RING: open by staking a concrete evidentiary tension as 'the organizing puzzle', close by resolving it verbatim; final sentence refuses to forecast and positions the review above both sides of the public debate. NO meta-commentary opening ('This report synthesizes the retrieved research evidence...' is deleted from every template). (11) SECTOR TABLE: 8 sectors x dominant technology generation x primary finding x anchor studies — the instruction-coverage matrix rendered as content; thin cells narrated as gap-findings. (12) METHODS TEXTURE mandatory on cited empirical claims: N, design, identification, in prose from card fields.
EXPECTED: Honest sum from 0.4382, per-dimension, weighted (insight .32/compreh .29/instr .25/readability .14): READABILITY 0.3774 → ~0.520 (the board's observed ceiling — every non-POLARIS system sits 0.51-0.52; the fix is fully mechanical: 100w paragraphs, H3/H4, no walls) = +0.020 weighted. INSIGHT 0.4238 → ~0.555 (the four-class synthesis lane + the dedicated insight section + adjudication density + thesis; targets cellcog's own mechanism at its own dimension, cellcog=0.5708) = +0.042 weighted. COMPREHENSIVENESS 0.4549 → ~0.555 (120-150 on-topic journal works vs cellcog ~100, 13k body words, methods texture on every cited claim, sector matrix, recency through 2026 — the rubric's 'Data and Factual Support' and 'Depth and Representativeness' lanes, our genuine out-score lane) = +0.029 weighted. INSTRUCTION-FOLLOWING 0.4409 → ~0.552 (journal-only compliance argued in prose and at point of use, working-paper quarantine, sectors covered visibly, 4IR engaged-and-critiqued; cellcog=0.5530) = +0.028 weighted. TOTAL ≈ 0.4382 + 0.119 ≈ 0.557. VERDICT: this LANDS ON 0.5578, it does not safely BEAT it — the central estimate beats bodhi's measured 0.5441 by ~4x the resolvable effect, but beating 0.5578 by a resolvable margin (mean >= 0.567) depends on the wheel converting the comprehensiveness-and-verification edge (more sources, newer sources, visible methods texture cellcog cannot exceed without our corpus) into +0.01 above the first-pass estimate. That is what iterations 3+ exist for, and the honest statement is: first full pass ~0.545-0.557; beat-with-margin requires 2-4 wheel iterations and is plausible, not guaranteed.
CEILING: Three honesty items. (1) THE TARGET ITSELF IS SOFT: 0.5578 is cellcog's corpus-wide 100-task average; cellcog published no task-72 score. Regression across the 9 dual-scored systems puts its task-72 at ~0.556 with residual SD 0.0094 — the true target is ~0.556±0.019, possibly below bodhi's measured 0.5441, possibly ~0.575. We therefore run two thresholds: beat-best-measured (0.5441+margin, decidable) and beat-estimate (0.5578+margin, the stretch). (2) READABILITY IS CAPPED: nobody on the board exceeds ~0.52; the judge compresses this dim; budget no gains above it. (3) WHAT COULD KEEP US UNDER 0.5578: (a) the insight dimension may pay cellcog's 8 syntheses partly for genuine domain-expert quality, not just the labeled-hedged-gap FORM — if our class-4 sentences are formulaic, the judge's prose will say so and the ceiling is ~0.55; (b) retrieval may saturate below ~120 on-topic works if OA full-text coverage of 2024-2026 journals is poor — then comprehensiveness caps near parity with cellcog instead of above it; (c) cross-dimension bleed works both ways — a 13.5k-word report with any residual wall-of-text regressions taxes everything. If after the kill rule fires the plateau is 0.545-0.555, the honest report is: we beat every measured task-72 score on the board, and whether we beat cellcog is unmeasurable at judge noise — exactly the sentence the operator should get, not a proxy.

LENS:
BAR-LOWERING (the lens that caught every previous plan, including yours): does this plan actually aim at BEATING 0.5578, or has it retreated to parity/bodhi/"a solid improvement"? Quote where it settles, or confirm it does not.

attachment.content:
- deep-research: Deep research harness — fan-out web searches, fetch sources, adversarially verify claims, synthesize a cited report. - When the user wants a deep, multi-source, fact-checked research report on any topic. BEFORE invoking, check if the question is specific enough to research directly — if underspecified (e.g., "what car to buy" without budget/use-case/region), ask 2-3 clarifying questions to narrow scope. Then pass the refined question as args, weaving the answers in.
- dataviz: Use this skill whenever you are about to create ANY chart, graph, plot, dashboard, or data visualization, in ANY output medium — an HTML or React artifact, inline SVG, plotting code in any library (matplotlib, plotly, d3, Recharts, …), an image/PNG you will render and upload, or a chart shared into Slack. Read it BEFORE writing the first line of chart code, choosing chart colors, building a stat tile / meter / KPI row, or laying out a dashboard. Produces visualizations that read as one system — elegant, accessible, consistent in light and dark — using a brand-neutral placeholder palette you swap for your own. Teaches a design-system-agnostic method: a form heuristic, a color formula with a runnable validator, mark specs, and interaction rules. A validated default palette is documented in `references/palette.md` — swap that file's values for your brand's. Triggers on: "chart", "graph", "plot", "data viz", "visualization", "dashboard", "analytics", "visualize data", "categorical colors", "sequential / diverging palette", "stat tile", "sparkline", "heatmap", "legend", "axis", "tooltip", "chart colors", "color by series".
- update-config: Use this skill to configure the Claude Code harness via settings.json. Automated behaviors ("from now on when X", "each time X", "whenever X", "before/after X") require hooks configured in settings.json - the harness executes these, not Claude, so memory/preferences cannot fulfill them. Also use for: permissions ("allow X", "add permission", "move permission to"), env vars ("set X=Y"), hook troubleshooting, or any changes to settings.json/settings.local.json files. Examples: "allow npm commands", "add bq permission to global settings", "move permission to user settings", "set DEBUG=true", "when claude stops show X". For simple settings like theme/model, suggest the /config command.
- keybindings-help: Use when the user wants to customize keyboard shortcuts, rebind keys, add chord bindings, or modify ~/.claude/keybindings.json. Examples: "rebind ctrl+s", "add a chord shortcut", "change the submit key", "customize keybindings".
- verify: Verify that a code change actually does what it's supposed to by exercising it end-to-end and observing behavior — drive the affected flow, not just tests or typecheck. Run before committing nontrivial changes; bootstraps this repo's project verify skill if none exists yet. Don't invoke it on a diff that only touches tests, docs, or other code with no runtime surface to drive (a change to product source always has one) — there's nothing to observe.
- code-review: Review the current diff for correctness bugs and reuse/simplification/efficiency cleanups at the given effort level (low/medium: fewer, high-confidence findings; high→max: broader coverage, may include uncertain findings; ultra: deep multi-agent review in the cloud). Pass --comment to post findings as inline PR comments, or --fix to apply the findings to the working tree after the review.
- simplify: Review the changed code for reuse, simplification, efficiency, and altitude cleanups, then apply the fixes. Quality only — it does not hunt for bugs; use /code-review for that.
- fewer-permission-prompts: Scan your transcripts for common read-only Bash and MCP tool calls, then add a prioritized allowlist to project .claude/settings.json to reduce permission prompts.
- loop: Run a prompt or slash command on a recurring interval (e.g. /loop 5m /foo). Omit the interval to let the model self-pace. - When the user wants to set up a recurring task, poll for status, or run something repeatedly on an interval (e.g. "check the deploy every 5 minutes", "keep running /babysit-prs"). Do NOT invoke for one-off tasks.
- schedule: Create, update, list, or run scheduled cloud agents (routines) that execute on a cron schedule. - When the user wants to schedule a recurring cloud agent, set up automated tasks, create a cron job for Claude Code, or manage their scheduled agents/routines. Also use when the user wants a one-time scheduled run ("run this once at 3pm", "remind me to check X tomorrow").
- claude-api: Reference for the Claude API / Anthropic SDK — model ids, pricing, params, streaming, tool use, MCP, agents, caching, token counting, model migration.
TRIGGER — read BEFORE opening the target file; don't skip because it "looks like a one-liner" — whenever: the prompt names Claude/Anthropic in any form (Claude, Anthropic, Fable, Opus, Sonnet, Haiku, `anthropic`, `@anthropic-ai`, `claude-*`, `us.anthropic.*`, `[1m]`); the user asks about an LLM (pricing/model choice/limits/caching) — never answer from memory; OR the task is LLM-shaped with provider unstated (agent/MCP/tool-definition/multi-agent/RAG/LLM-judge/computer-use; generate/summarize/extract/classify/rewrite/converse over NL; debugging refusals/cutoffs/streaming/tool-calls/tokens).
SKIP only when another provider is being worked on (overrides all triggers): OpenAI/GPT/Gemini/Llama/Mistral/Cohere/Ollama named in the query; OR `grep -rE 'openai|langchain_openai|google.generativeai|genai|mistralai|cohere|ollama'` over the project hits (run this grep FIRST if no provider named — don't Read the file).
- run: Launch and drive this project's app to see a change working. Use when asked to run, start, or screenshot the app, or to confirm a change works in the real app (not just tests). First looks for a project skill that already covers launching the app; otherwise falls back to built-in patterns per project type (CLI, server, TUI, Electron, browser-driven, library).
- init: Initialize a new CLAUDE.md file with codebase documentation
- review: Review a GitHub pull request; for your working diff use /code-review
- security-review: Complete a security review of the pending changes on the current branch

content.text: Board data verified: `/home/polaris/polaris_project/drb_corpus/gpt55_board/leaderboard_gpt55.csv` and `/home/polaris/polaris_project/drb_corpus/gpt55_board/scores/cellcog-max/` (only `race_result.txt`, no `raw_results.jsonl` — the plan's "units" claim is factually correct). But under the BAR-LOWERING lens the plan fails, and it fails in its own arithmetic, not just its rhetoric.

input.reasoning:
REFUTED under the BAR-LOWERING lens. The plan does not aim at beating 0.5578; it aims at LANDING ON it, installs a lower fallback threshold, and pre-writes the excuse for missing.

THE ARITHMETIC IS THE CATCH — every dimension target is AT OR BELOW cellcog's published dimension score.
cellcog (leaderboard_gpt55.csv, verified): comprehensiveness .5634 / insight .5708 / instruction .5530 / readability .5194.
The plan's EXPECTED block:
- INSIGHT -> ~0.555  vs cellcog .5708  = BELOW
- COMPREHENSIVENESS -> ~0.555  vs cellcog .5634  = BELOW
- INSTRUCTION -> ~0.552  vs cellcog .5530  = BELOW
- READABILITY -> ~0.520  vs cellcog .5194  = parity, explicitly capped by fiat
You cannot exceed a weighted average while sitting at or under it on all four components. The plan's own numbers therefore make beating cellcog IMPOSSIBLE BY CONSTRUCTION; the 0.557 total only "reaches" 0.5578 by rounding. It never nominates a single dimension where it intends to EXCEED cellcog's measured dimension score.

The contradiction is quotable and internal: the plan calls comprehensiveness "our genuine out-score lane" ("120-150 on-topic journal works vs cellcog ~100... evidence volume is the lane where we out-score rather than match") and then sets that lane's target at 0.555 — BELOW cellcog's 0.5634. It plans 1.3-1.5x the evidence and budgets a LOWER score than the system it is out-evidencing. That is the bar being lowered inside the very lane the plan declares its edge.

WHERE IT SETTLES — verbatim:
1. "VERDICT: this LANDS ON 0.5578, it does not safely BEAT it."
2. "We therefore run two thresholds: beat-best-measured (0.5441+margin, decidable) and beat-estimate (0.5578+margin, the stretch)." — This is bodhi reinstalled as an acceptable success criterion. The brief's first line: "TARGET: > 0.5578 (cellcog-max, #1). NOT parity. NOT bodhi. THE TOP." The plan re-admits the exact fallback the brief banned, and labels the real target "the stretch."
3. "first full pass ~0.545-0.557; beat-with-margin requires 2-4 wheel iterations and is plausible, not guaranteed." — the first pass's own midpoint (0.551) is below the target.
4. "the honest report is: we beat every measured task-72 score on the board, and whether we beat cellcog is unmeasurable at judge noise" — a PRE-WRITTEN VICTORY STATEMENT for a run that misses the target. The plan has already drafted what it will say when it fails.

THE SOFT-TARGET MOVE IS THE BAR-LOWERING MECHANISM. The CEILING section re-derives cellcog's task-72 as "~0.556 +/- 0.019, possibly below bodhi's measured 0.5441." The unit correction is factually right (cellcog ships only race_result.txt, no per-task rows — I verified). But the plan uses that correct fact to CONVERT the goal from a fixed number into a distribution wide enough that a 0.545-0.557 outcome can be declared a win. Correct diagnosis, weaponized as an alibi. Note the asymmetry: the same regression says cellcog's task-72 could be ~0.575 — and the plan budgets nothing for that branch.

READABILITY CAPPED BY FIAT. "READABILITY IS CAPPED: nobody on the board exceeds ~0.52... budget no gains above it." A ceiling inferred from 10 observations of systems that all write the same way, applied to the one dimension the plan itself calls "fully mechanical" (100w paragraphs, H3/H4, no walls). If the fix is mechanical and the field is undifferentiated (all 10 systems sit 0.457-0.532), that is the cheapest place to actually EXCEED #1 — and the plan pre-emptively forbids trying. Cost of assuming the cap and being wrong: ~0 (we do the same work). Cost of assuming it and being right: nothing lost. So the cap has no decision value; it exists only to shrink the expected total to a number the plan can defend.

WHAT A NON-BAR-LOWERING PLAN WOULD LOOK LIKE. Same machinery, retargeted: every dimension aimed ABOVE cellcog's published number (insight >= .58 not .555; comprehensiveness >= .58 not .555; instruction >= .57; readability attacked to .54+ rather than conceded at .52), one threshold only (0.5578 + margin), and no fallback narrative. The mechanisms in RETRIEVAL/SYNTHESIS/COMPOSER are mostly sound and mostly aimed at COPYING cellcog (copy the insight engine, copy the monograph architecture, copy the citation template, copy the era spine). A plan built almost entirely from "copy #1" has a natural ceiling of #1 — the only additive lever is evidence volume, and the plan then under-budgets that lever below #1's own score. That is why the arithmetic comes out at a tie: the design IS a tie.

Default was refuted=true if uncertain; here there is no uncertainty. The plan states the retreat itself.
input.fix:
Three changes, none of which touch the retrieval/synthesis/composer machinery (which is fine) — only the target-setting, which is where the retreat lives:

1. DELETE THE SECOND THRESHOLD. Strike "beat-best-measured (0.5441+margin)" and the sentence "we beat every measured task-72 score on the board, and whether we beat cellcog is unmeasurable at judge noise." One threshold: mean >= 0.5578 + margin. A plan that ships with a fallback success criterion will hit the fallback.

2. RETARGET EVERY DIMENSION ABOVE cellcog's PUBLISHED SCORE, and make each an owned lever:
   - comprehensiveness .4549 -> >= 0.58 (cellcog .5634). This is the declared out-score lane; budget it as one. 150+ on-topic journal works, >=35% 2024+, methods texture (N/design/identification) on every cited empirical claim — cellcog cannot match this without our corpus.
   - insight .4238 -> >= 0.58 (cellcog .5708). Do not just replicate cellcog's 8 named syntheses; ship 10-12 AND ground a subset in the contradiction index (a synthesis that ADJUDICATES two cited findings is strictly stronger to a judge than one that only declares a gap). That is the one insight move cellcog structurally cannot make: it has no verified card corpus to adjudicate from.
   - instruction .4409 -> >= 0.57 (cellcog .5530).
   - readability .3774 -> attack to >= 0.54, do NOT concede at 0.52. The plan itself says the fix is mechanical; the 0.52 "cap" is 10 samples of systems that all write in walls. Cost of trying is zero.
   Target total then reads ~0.575, with the tie at 0.5578 as the DOWNSIDE case, not the plan.

3. NAME THE ASYMMETRIC BRANCH. The same regression that says cellcog's task-72 might be 0.556 says it might be 0.575. Plan for 0.575. If the true value is lower, we win by more; if the plan is built for 0.5578 and the truth is 0.575, we lose and call it noise — which is precisely the outcome the current CEILING section is pre-authorizing.
